  ],
  "text": "The Node.js ESM (ECMAScript Module) loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge is implemented across multiple layers:\n\n## Entry Point Detection\nThe entry point ESM detection begins in `lib/internal/modules/run_main.js`, which contains the `shouldUseESMLoader()` function that determines whether the main module should be loaded as ESM based on factors like `--experimental-default-type`, presence of `.mjs` extensions, and custom loaders. The `resolveMainPath()` function resolves the absolute path to the main entry point.\n\n## Main ESM Loader\nThe core ESM loader is the `ModuleLoader` class in `lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js`. This class manages:\n- Module resolution via the `resolve()` method (which delegates to `defaultResolve()` from resolve.js)\n- Module loading via the `load()` method (which delegates to `defaultLoad()` from load.js)\n- Module job creation and caching via `getModuleJobForImport()`\n- Custom loader hooks support through the `CustomizedModuleLoader` and `HooksProxy` classes\n\n## Module Job Pipeline\nThe `ModuleJob` class in `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js` manages the loading, linking, instantiation, and evaluation lifecycle:\n- The `_link()` method resolves all module dependencies\n- The `instantiate()` method triggers module instantiation via ModuleWrap\n- The `run()` method evaluates the module\n\nA synchronous variant, `ModuleJobSync`, is used for `require()` in imported CJS modules.\n\n## Caching\nThe `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` classes in `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_map.js` cache resolution and loading results respectively, maintaining the invariant that identical module requests always resolve to the same result per ECMAScript spec.\n\n## ESM Format Translators\nThe `translators` map in `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js` contains translator functions for different module formats:\n- 'module' - Standard JavaScript modules\n- 'commonjs' - CommonJS modules loaded as ESM\n- 'commonjs-sync' - Synchronous CommonJS variant\n- 'builtin' - Node.js built-in modules\n- 'json' - JSON modules\n- 'wasm' - WebAssembly modules\n\nEach translator converts raw source into a `ModuleWrap` object.\n\n## ESM Specifier Resolution\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js` module implements ESM resolution:\n- `defaultResolve()` - Main resolution function\n- `packageExportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json exports\n- `packageImportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json imports\n- Various resolution helpers for handling package target resolution and conditions\n\n## Module Loading\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js` module implements loading:\n- `defaultLoad()` - Asynchronous loading of module source\n- `defaultLoadSync()` - Synchronous loading for require() in imported CJS\n- `getSource()` and `getSourceSync()` - Fetch module source from various protocols\n\n## CJS-ESM Interop\nThe `finalizeEsmResolution()` function in `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js` bridges CJS and ESM by finalizing ESM resolution results when CJS code loads ESM modules via package.json exports/imports resolution.\n\n## Native ModuleWrap C++ Binding\nThe native `ModuleWrap` class in `src/module_wrap.h` and `src/module_wrap.cc` provides the V8 integration layer:\n- `ModuleWrap::Link()` - Links module dependencies by connecting specifiers to resolved modules\n- `ModuleWrap::Instantiate()` - Instantiates the V8 module (resolves all dependencies)\n- `ModuleWrap::Evaluate()` - Evaluates the module and executes its code\n- `ModuleWrap::GetStatus()` - Returns the module's status (uninstantiated, instantiating, instantiated, evaluating, evaluated, errored)\n- `ModuleWrap::GetError()` - Returns any error from evaluation\n- `ModuleWrap::GetNamespace()` - Returns the module's namespace object\n- `ModuleWrap::GetModuleRequests()` - Returns the list of module dependencies\n\nAdditional ESM infrastructure:\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/initialize_import_meta.js` - Initializes import.meta object with url, dirname, filename, and resolve\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/get_format.js` - Determines module format based on file extension, mime type, and package.json\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/utils.js` - Utility functions including `compileSourceTextModule()` which creates ModuleWrap objects and `getDefaultConditions()` for package resolution conditions\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js` - Contains `HooksProxy` class for managing custom loader hooks communication"
}

conversation_events.text:
# Node.js CommonJS to ESM Module Loader Migration Surface

## Your Task

Find all JavaScript and C++ source files in nodejs/node that implement the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge. Identify: the ModuleLoader class (lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js), the CJS loader's ESM fallback (finalizeEsmResolution in lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js), the ESM translators for different formats, the ESM specifier resolver, the ModuleJob linking/evaluation pipeline, the entry point ESM detection (run_main.js), and the native ModuleWrap C++ binding.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/node--v22.13.0.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/node--v22.13.0` (nodejs/node)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop files in the nodejs/node repository. Let me start by exploring the workspace structure and then locate the specific files.
Now let me create a comprehensive answer.json file with all the findings about the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge:

Perfect! I've successfully created `/workspace/answer.json` with comprehensive findings about the Node.js ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Components Identified:

### **JavaScript ESM Loader Files:**
1. **lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js** - Main `ModuleLoader` class managing resolution, loading, and module job creation
2. **lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js** - `ModuleJob` and `ModuleJobSync` classes for the linking/evaluation pipeline
3. **lib/internal/modules/esm/module_map.js** - `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` classes for caching
4. **lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js** - ESM format translators for module, commonjs, json, wasm, and builtin formats
5. **lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js** - ESM specifier resolver with `defaultResolve()`, `packageExportsResolve()`, and `packageImportsResolve()` functions
6. **lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js** - Module loading with `defaultLoad()` and `defaultLoadSync()` functions
7. **lib/internal/modules/esm/initialize_import_meta.js** - `initializeImportMeta()` for import.meta initialization
8. **lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js** - `HooksProxy` class for custom loader hooks

### **Entry Point Detection:**
**lib/internal/modules/run_main.js** - Contains `shouldUseESMLoader()` and `resolveMainPath()` functions to determine if main module should be loaded as ESM

### **CJS-ESM Interop Bridge:**
**lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js** - Contains `finalizeEsmResolution()` function that bridges CJS and ESM resolution

### **Native C++ Binding:**
- **src/module_wrap.h** - `ModuleWrap` class definition
- **src/module_wrap.cc** - Implementation with methods: `Link()`, `Instantiate()`, `Evaluate()`, `GetStatus()`, `GetError()`, `GetNamespace()`, and `GetModuleRequests()`

The answer file includes all relevant files, key symbols, and a detailed narrative explaining the ESM loader architecture and how it integrates with CJS loading.

    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::Evaluate"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::GetStatus"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::GetError"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::GetNamespace"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::GetModuleRequests"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/run_main.js", "symbol": "shouldUseESMLoader"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js", "symbol": "createModuleLoader"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js", "symbol": "ModuleLoader"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js", "symbol": "getModuleJobForImport"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js", "symbol": "ModuleJob"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js", "symbol": "_link"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::Link"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js", "symbol": "instantiate"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::Instantiate"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js", "symbol": "run"},
    {"repo": "nodejs/node", "path": "src/module_wrap.cc", "symbol": "ModuleWrap::Evaluate"}
  ],
  "text": "The Node.js ESM (ECMAScript Module) loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge is implemented across multiple layers:\n\n## Entry Point Detection\nThe entry point ESM detection begins in `lib/internal/modules/run_main.js`, which contains the `shouldUseESMLoader()` function that determines whether the main module should be loaded as ESM based on factors like `--experimental-default-type`, presence of `.mjs` extensions, and custom loaders. The `resolveMainPath()` function resolves the absolute path to the main entry point.\n\n## Main ESM Loader\nThe core ESM loader is the `ModuleLoader` class in `lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js`. This class manages:\n- Module resolution via the `resolve()` method (which delegates to `defaultResolve()` from resolve.js)\n- Module loading via the `load()` method (which delegates to `defaultLoad()` from load.js)\n- Module job creation and caching via `getModuleJobForImport()`\n- Custom loader hooks support through the `CustomizedModuleLoader` and `HooksProxy` classes\n\n## Module Job Pipeline\nThe `ModuleJob` class in `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js` manages the loading, linking, instantiation, and evaluation lifecycle:\n- The `_link()` method resolves all module dependencies\n- The `instantiate()` method triggers module instantiation via ModuleWrap\n- The `run()` method evaluates the module\n\nA synchronous variant, `ModuleJobSync`, is used for `require()` in imported CJS modules.\n\n## Caching\nThe `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` classes in `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_map.js` cache resolution and loading results respectively, maintaining the invariant that identical module requests always resolve to the same result per ECMAScript spec.\n\n## ESM Format Translators\nThe `translators` map in `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js` contains translator functions for different module formats:\n- 'module' - Standard JavaScript modules\n- 'commonjs' - CommonJS modules loaded as ESM\n- 'commonjs-sync' - Synchronous CommonJS variant\n- 'builtin' - Node.js built-in modules\n- 'json' - JSON modules\n- 'wasm' - WebAssembly modules\n\nEach translator converts raw source into a `ModuleWrap` object.\n\n## ESM Specifier Resolution\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js` module implements ESM resolution:\n- `defaultResolve()` - Main resolution function\n- `packageExportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json exports\n- `packageImportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json imports\n- Various resolution helpers for handling package target resolution and conditions\n\n## Module Loading\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js` module implements loading:\n- `defaultLoad()` - Asynchronous loading of module source\n- `defaultLoadSync()` - Synchronous loading for require() in imported CJS\n- `getSource()` and `getSourceSync()` - Fetch module source from various protocols\n\n## CJS-ESM Interop\nThe `finalizeEsmResolution()` function in `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js` bridges CJS and ESM by finalizing ESM resolution results when CJS code loads ESM modules via package.json exports/imports resolution.\n\n## Native ModuleWrap C++ Binding\nThe native `ModuleWrap` class in `src/module_wrap.h` and `src/module_wrap.cc` provides the V8 integration layer:\n- `ModuleWrap::Link()` - Links module dependencies by connecting specifiers to resolved modules\n- `ModuleWrap::Instantiate()` - Instantiates the V8 module (resolves all dependencies)\n- `ModuleWrap::Evaluate()` - Evaluates the module and executes its code\n- `ModuleWrap::GetStatus()` - Returns the module's status (uninstantiated, instantiating, instantiated, evaluating, evaluated, errored)\n- `ModuleWrap::GetError()` - Returns any error from evaluation\n- `ModuleWrap::GetNamespace()` - Returns the module's namespace object\n- `ModuleWrap::GetModuleRequests()` - Returns the list of module dependencies\n\nAdditional ESM infrastructure:\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/initialize_import_meta.js` - Initializes import.meta object with url, dirname, filename, and resolve\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/get_format.js` - Determines module format based on file extension, mime type, and package.json\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/utils.js` - Utility functions including `compileSourceTextModule()` which creates ModuleWrap objects and `getDefaultConditions()` for package resolution conditions\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js` - Contains `HooksProxy` class for managing custom loader hooks communication"
}

  "text": "The Node.js ESM (ECMAScript Module) loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge is implemented across multiple layers:\n\n## Entry Point Detection\nThe entry point ESM detection begins in `lib/internal/modules/run_main.js`, which contains the `shouldUseESMLoader()` function that determines whether the main module should be loaded as ESM based on factors like `--experimental-default-type`, presence of `.mjs` extensions, and custom loaders. The `resolveMainPath()` function resolves the absolute path to the main entry point.\n\n## Main ESM Loader\nThe core ESM loader is the `ModuleLoader` class in `lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js`. This class manages:\n- Module resolution via the `resolve()` method (which delegates to `defaultResolve()` from resolve.js)\n- Module loading via the `load()` method (which delegates to `defaultLoad()` from load.js)\n- Module job creation and caching via `getModuleJobForImport()`\n- Custom loader hooks support through the `CustomizedModuleLoader` and `HooksProxy` classes\n\n## Module Job Pipeline\nThe `ModuleJob` class in `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js` manages the loading, linking, instantiation, and evaluation lifecycle:\n- The `_link()` method resolves all module dependencies\n- The `instantiate()` method triggers module instantiation via ModuleWrap\n- The `run()` method evaluates the module\n\nA synchronous variant, `ModuleJobSync`, is used for `require()` in imported CJS modules.\n\n## Caching\nThe `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` classes in `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_map.js` cache resolution and loading results respectively, maintaining the invariant that identical module requests always resolve to the same result per ECMAScript spec.\n\n## ESM Format Translators\nThe `translators` map in `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js` contains translator functions for different module formats:\n- 'module' - Standard JavaScript modules\n- 'commonjs' - CommonJS modules loaded as ESM\n- 'commonjs-sync' - Synchronous CommonJS variant\n- 'builtin' - Node.js built-in modules\n- 'json' - JSON modules\n- 'wasm' - WebAssembly modules\n\nEach translator converts raw source into a `ModuleWrap` object.\n\n## ESM Specifier Resolution\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js` module implements ESM resolution:\n- `defaultResolve()` - Main resolution function\n- `packageExportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json exports\n- `packageImportsResolve()` - Resolves package.json imports\n- Various resolution helpers for handling package target resolution and conditions\n\n## Module Loading\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js` module implements loading:\n- `defaultLoad()` - Asynchronous loading of module source\n- `defaultLoadSync()` - Synchronous loading for require() in imported CJS\n- `getSource()` and `getSourceSync()` - Fetch module source from various protocols\n\n## CJS-ESM Interop\nThe `finalizeEsmResolution()` function in `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js` bridges CJS and ESM by finalizing ESM resolution results when CJS code loads ESM modules via package.json exports/imports resolution.\n\n## Native ModuleWrap C++ Binding\nThe native `ModuleWrap` class in `src/module_wrap.h` and `src/module_wrap.cc` provides the V8 integration layer:\n- `ModuleWrap::Link()` - Links module dependencies by connecting specifiers to resolved modules\n- `ModuleWrap::Instantiate()` - Instantiates the V8 module (resolves all dependencies)\n- `ModuleWrap::Evaluate()` - Evaluates the module and executes its code\n- `ModuleWrap::GetStatus()` - Returns the module's status (uninstantiated, instantiating, instantiated, evaluating, evaluated, errored)\n- `ModuleWrap::GetError()` - Returns any error from evaluation\n- `ModuleWrap::GetNamespace()` - Returns the module's namespace object\n- `ModuleWrap::GetModuleRequests()` - Returns the list of module dependencies\n\nAdditional ESM infrastructure:\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/initialize_import_meta.js` - Initializes import.meta object with url, dirname, filename, and resolve\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/get_format.js` - Determines module format based on file extension, mime type, and package.json\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/utils.js` - Utility functions including `compileSourceTextModule()` which creates ModuleWrap objects and `getDefaultConditions()` for package resolution conditions\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js` - Contains `HooksProxy` class for managing custom loader hooks communication"
}
", "structuredPatch": [], "originalFile": null}
